sanskrit: एतान्न हन्तुमिच्छामि घ्नतोऽपि मधुसूदन | अपि त्रैलोक्यराज्यस्य हेतोः किं नु महीकृते ||
transliteration: etān na hantum icchāmi ghnato ’pi madhusūdana | api trailokya-rājyasya hetoḥ kiṃ nu mahī-kṛte ||
literal_meaning: These—even if they kill—I do not wish to kill, O Madhusūdana. Even for the three-worlds’ kingdom, why speak of earth?
contextual_meaning: Arjuna refuses to fight even in self-defence, devaluing every worldly gain because the cost is the death of loved ones; he experiences acute moral injury and existential collapse.
psychological_state: Moral injury with survivor-guilt and depressive catastrophising; protective avoidance of aggression.
trigger_scenario: A whistle-blower asked to testify against corrupt colleagues who once mentored him, choosing unemployment over betrayal.
anchor_text: Love can paralyze the warrior when the enemy wears a familiar face.
